whatsNew: One grid, and a lock that carries intent. The engine's two spacing canons (Golden Rule 5 said 8px; the component rules and skills said 6px multiples) are unified: 6px is now the single spatial grid everywhere — Golden Rule 5, the Quality Gate, VISUAL-CRAFT CR-1's scale, hero card padding (p-8 → p-9), and /ss-dial's density ramp (now a clean 36/24/18/12px ladder). A11y floors are untouched (≥44px touch targets, ≥8px between adjacent targets — that's WCAG spacing, not the grid). And the Design Lock gains Brand intent (concept ported from google-labs-code/design.md): Reference · Implied traits · Never · Feeling — ONE concrete reference anchored by the 3 traits it implies plus 3 negative constraints; a bare reference or bare adjectives are both forbidden. /ss-setup captures it, /ss-page and /ss-component read the lock first and obey it. Plus: /ss-setup's lock template caught up with the engine lock (asks the surface, proposes the Mood 4-axes, derives the Motion seed; lock gains Surface/Mood/Type scale, Type renamed to Font); /ss-lint gains a WCAG token-contrast check (bundled stdlib-only contrast_check.py — deterministic, never mental gamma math); /ss-update gains a changed-rule gate (a rule whose VALUE changed, like 8px→6px, is surfaced old→new with a per-item OK, never bundled into a blanket Y). And the engine eats its own dog food: button/chip labels are tokenized as --brand-foreground (no more hardcoded white — fixes vercel-dark's white-on-white brand button) and all seven skins now pass the contrast check in both scopes.
update: Run /ss-update, or: npx skills add bitjaru/styleseed
